findings.original_claim: Append-oriented authoritative log with replay recovery
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: AppendRecord stores seq/command_kind/generation only; replay_log validates sequence and returns checkpoint state unchanged
findings.required_action: Implement command payload in log and deterministic replay, or reclassify candidate as checkpoint-snapshot bundle

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Compaction truncates log and bumps generation without resetting committed_seq; replay_log expects seq from 1
findings.required_action: Fix seq/generation contract after compaction or add explicit recovery repair path

findings.original_claim: Single-writer enforcement with crash recovery and takeover
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Writer token stored in DB row or manifest; cleared only on close; no takeover or abandoned-writer recovery
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: ProcessCrashRecovery single-writer correctness; MD-015 crash-held-writer experiments
findings.required_action: Add stale-writer recovery with integrity validation; do not rely on PID absence

findings.original_claim: WAL-safe duplication and backup via session.db copy
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL enabled; duplicate_session uses fs::copy of session.db only without -wal/-shm or backup API
findings.required_action: Use SQLite backup API or checkpoint-and-copy protocol; test duplication while source writer is open

findings.original_claim: Material distinctness: relational transactional authority vs append-dominant log
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Both candidates persist full normalized JSON snapshots (SQLite state_json row vs checkpoint file); append log is non-authoritative stub
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: Candidate material distinctness for mechanism comparison
findings.required_action: Rewrite candidate class declarations to match actual authority models or rebuild mechanisms

findings.original_claim: Durable acknowledgement after append fsync and atomic manifest rename with directory fsync
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Only append log path calls sync_all; checkpoint and manifest use temp write + rename without file or directory fsync
findings.required_action: Add durable sync for checkpoint, manifest, and parent directory where supported

findings.original_claim: Relational canonical entity storage with integrity check
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Canonical state is single state_json blob; no PRAGMA integrity_check; tables are meta/lock/derived shell
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: Embedded transactional database with relational authority
findings.required_action: Reclassify as SQLite-hosted JSON snapshot or implement relational decomposition

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Derived artifacts corrupted but open/read paths load canonical only; derived never consulted or rebuilt; oracle compares fixture to actual
findings.unsupported_higher_claim: Derived corruption classification and rebuild semantics
findings.required_action: Assert derived detect/rebuild behavior on reopen or downgrade scenario claim

findings.original_claim: Independent session identity with source lineage and oracle-valid duplication
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Oracle compares duplicate.normalized_state to itself; no persisted reopen of source or duplicate; adapter-return-only check
findings.required_action: Reopen source and duplicate from disk; compare against independent expected state; verify source unchanged

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: FailBeforeDurabilityCommit fires before any write/transaction; reopen proves unchanged state trivially
findings.required_action: Add mid-write and post-ack fault points; classify as logical pre-commit abort until fixed

findings.original_claim: Interrupted compaction does not expose mixed canonical state
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Pass on simulated-compaction-interrupt error with fixture-to-fixture oracle; no reopen or post-fault state validation
findings.required_action: Reopen after interrupt and validate state/oracle; invalidate current Passed classification

findings.original_claim: At most one authoritative writer under concurrent access
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Second open rejected with writer-already-open; oracle self-compares before to before; no crash/orphan lock recovery
findings.required_action: Replace self-compare; add orphan-lock and crash-held-writer recovery scenarios

findings.original_claim: Semantic oracle pass for corruption and format scenarios
findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: passed_oracle(fixture, fixture) tautology; canonical corruption accepts any Err
findings.required_action: Use independent expected observation or non-oracle pass criteria; require specific error codes

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: 13 Passed scenarios include tautological oracle passes and logical-only faults; class declarations exceed implementation
findings.required_action: Downgrade eligibility to catalog-pass only; selection_status remains none

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Same-handle read after apply; no close-to-reopen cycle; expected built with shared apply_command
findings.required_action: Add close-to-reopen after every persistence claim; separate expected oracle from adapter apply path

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Single-handle stale precondition rejection with before/after compare; no multi-actor concurrency
findings.required_action: Reclassify failure_model to scoped-precondition; retain as valid lower-strength evidence

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: Unsupported due to missing capability; correctly retained as negative/unsupported
findings.required_action: Retain Unsupported status; implement only after destructive GC capability exists

findings.observed_implementation_or_assertion: FaultLayer::Logical only; limitations.json records hardware_power_loss not tested; no fsync-at-fault injection
findings.required_action: Add filesystem-layer fault injection and platform evidence before claiming durability
